merge_decisions.reason: Both are event-ledger/retrain outputs under next_adverse_xslip10bps and already de-overlap by (signal_start, side).
merge_decisions.next_action: Keep low_eff_rf_rank_median_000 and ctx4h_scaled025 as the current ETH-local falsification set.

merge_decisions.decision: merge the framework and keep strict lifecycle as the comparison contract
merge_decisions.reason: T3 structure is mutually exclusive with T2 and uses compatible features; the bridge replay now prevents adding adverse10 ledger returns to lifecycle returns.
merge_decisions.next_action: Use the strict lifecycle union row as the research comparison surface; keep adverse10 ledgers as diagnostic provenance only.

merge_decisions.reason: Historical reentry_window allowed optimistic same-second/non-cross re_p fills; strict replay invalidated the large T3 contribution.
merge_decisions.next_action: Treat Task 14-17 historical T3 positive results as suspect-only provenance.

merge_decisions.reason: It improves strict T3 split and survives final-mark exclusion, but increases exposure and total strict lifecycle remains negative.
merge_decisions.next_action: Keep it inside strict lifecycle union tests only; do not promote it to live stop semantics.

merge_decisions.reason: The two risk-shaping layers stack under strict_next_second_cross, but the combined fixed-calendar lifecycle remains negative.
merge_decisions.next_action: Attack residual T2 loss and BTC drag before any live/default discussion.

merge_decisions.decision: zero-fail exposure is the best strict research row; skip-fail is the cleaner executable challenger
merge_decisions.reason: Reducing failed-context original_t2 exposure improves total lifecycle and worst silo; skip-fail keeps most of the improvement without relying on zero-notional lock occupancy.
merge_decisions.next_action: Use skip-fail as the cleaner next implementation surface, while retaining scaled000 as the upper-bound research control.

merge_decisions.decision: reject current original_t2 pass bucket as a trading leg
merge_decisions.reason: Strict lifecycle attribution shows all simple pass buckets remain fee-negative; disabling original_t2 beats ctx4h skip-fail and pre_touch<=900.
merge_decisions.next_action: Do not spend more sweeps on ctx4h/pass timing knobs; either disable original_t2 or implement an exact event-time probability/RF lifecycle hook.

merge_decisions.reason: The RF-selected event set can be injected as explicit locks, but only a minority become strict reentry trades and the external leg is still net negative.
merge_decisions.next_action: Use RF only with a post-touch entry/confirmation redesign; do not treat the adverse10 event-ledger result as lifecycle-ready.

contract_readiness.blocker: No executable lifecycle breakout-lock rule yet; current row is additive ledger selection.
contract_readiness.next_action: Translate the selected low-eff/RF leg into a replay-time original_t2 quality gate or keep it ledger-only.

contract_readiness.current_contract: T2 next_adverse_xslip10bps event ledger with derived scaled sizing
contract_readiness.blocker: Bridge hook has a full fixed-calendar lifecycle result for the original_t2 ctx4h scaled-sizing approximation; exact event-ledger parity is still pending.
contract_readiness.next_action: Use the lifecycle bridge result to decide whether ctx4h sizing deserves exact parity work or should be rejected.

contract_readiness.blocker: None for T3 baseline; it is already the strict lifecycle control.
contract_readiness.next_action: Use as lifecycle control, not as a profitability benchmark for adverse10 T2 ledgers.

contract_readiness.current_contract: baseline_plus_t3 strict_next_second_cross lifecycle with T3-only exit override
contract_readiness.blocker: Watch-only because total lifecycle remains negative and exposure risk must stay visible.
contract_readiness.next_action: Only compare against T2 once T2 expansion is replayed through the same lifecycle contract.
